text.title: Agent Rules and Gotchas
text.summary: Critical agent rules for xUnit test generation: async signatures, non-determinism, coverage loop, and legal patterns.

rule.statement: All [Fact] and [Theory] test methods must return async Task, never async void.
rule.rationale: xUnit cannot await async void tests; exceptions are swallowed, tests appear to pass when they should fail.

rule.statement: Replace DateTime.UtcNow with an IClock interface before generating timestamp assertions.
rule.rationale: Tests asserting on real timestamps are non-deterministic and produce flaky Assert.Equal failures on slow CI runners.

rule.statement: Reject any generated test that uses Thread.Sleep or polling. Use TaskCompletionSource or IHostApplicationLifetime instead.
rule.rationale: Sleep-based tests are timing-sensitive and create random CI failures that are hard to reproduce locally.

rule.statement: Use Assert.Equal(expected, actual) form; never Assert.True(expected == actual).
rule.rationale: Assert.True without a message produces "Expected: True, Actual: False" — no information about what failed.

rule.statement: Run coverlet after each test pass. For lines with hit count 0, emit one new [Theory] + [InlineData] case per uncovered branch, then re-run until line coverage threshold is met.
rule.rationale: Systematic coverage-driven generation avoids the common pattern of writing many tests for the same happy path while leaving error branches uncovered.

example.note: Threshold=80 blocks the test run and returns exit code 2 if line coverage drops below 80%. Agents use this as a hard quality gate.

antipattern.description: Generating _mock.Setup(...) for every dependency method including ones the SUT never calls.
antipattern.reason: Over-specified mocks make tests break when refactoring adds or removes method calls that are not part of the public contract.

antipattern.description: Generating duplicate [InlineData] rows with the same logical case under different labels.
antipattern.reason: Duplicates inflate test count without adding coverage signal; they hide the actual set of cases being exercised.

antipattern.description: Using WebApplicationFactory without public partial class Program {} in .NET 6+ minimal hosting.
antipattern.reason: The test assembly cannot see Program unless it is public or has InternalsVisibleTo set; integration tests fail to compile silently.
